title: Color-Coded Inbound and Outbound Relationships in ERD
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing an ERD, I want to see color-coded inbound and outbound relationships when I click an entity, so that I can quickly distinguish primary keys from foreign keys and trace data flow.
description:
## Context
The ERD diagram shows relationships between entities but does not visually differentiate between inbound (primary key) and outbound (foreign key) relationships.

## Behavior
- **Foreign keys** are displayed with a green icon and green relationship lines indicating outbound references.
- **Primary keys** are displayed with a blue icon and blue relationship lines indicating inbound references.
- Clicking an object node highlights both its inbound and outbound relationships simultaneously, making it easy to trace the full relationship graph for a selected entity.
acceptance_criteria: Foreign key fields are flagged with a green icon in the object table
Foreign key relationship lines render in green
Primary key fields are flagged with a blue icon in the object table
Primary key (inbound) relationship lines render in blue
Clicking an object node highlights both inbound and outbound relationships
Color coding is visually distinct and accessible
ux_changes: ERD diagram uses color-coded relationship lines: green for foreign key (outbound) links and blue for primary key (inbound) links. Clicking an entity node highlights all connected relationships in both directions.
implementation_notes: GoJS link templates updated to support conditional coloring based on relationship direction. Node click handler triggers highlight of all connected links.
